entries.d: To change the colour of your locks using a special product
entries.s: I really want to _____ bright pink this summer for fun — I'll use permanent colour to change it from brown.

entries.d: To have a permanent picture or design put on your skin
entries.s: My older cousin _____ a small rose permanently on her arm for her 18th birthday at the tattoo parlour.

entries.w: to hang out in shopping centres
entries.d: To spend time doing nothing special in a mall
entries.s: Some bored teenagers like to _____ after school, walking around the shops and just meeting friends without buying anything.
mc: to study hard in shopping centres

entries.d: To roller-skate without wearing protection on your knees and elbows
entries.s: It's very dangerous to _____ without pads on your knees and elbows — you could hurt yourself badly if you fall down.
mc: to go roller-skating with full protective pads
to go for a walk without shoes
to sit on the sofa without cushions

entries.d: To have a small piece of jewellery put through your nostril
entries.s: She asked her parents nervously if she could _____ — a tiny piece of silver jewellery for her nose piercing.

entries.d: To move your finger up and down on your screen to look at things
entries.s: He spends hours every evening just _____ through his phone, looking at social media photos and videos without really doing anything.

entries.w: to have a party at home
entries.d: To invite friends over to celebrate at your place
entries.s: Can I please _____ a party at home for my 13th birthday? I want to invite 10 friends over for pizza and cake.

entries.d: To choose and pay for what you wear yourself
entries.s: My parents finally let me _____ my own clothes now with my pocket money instead of them choosing for me.

entries.d: To have more candy and sugar than is good for you
entries.s: The dentist strongly said I should stop _____ too many sweets like chocolate and candy, because they're bad for my teeth.

entries.d: To put on small pieces of jewellery on your ears
entries.s: She got her ears pierced last month at the jewellery shop and now she _____ every single day to school.

entries.w: to ride your bike without a helmet
entries.d: To cycle without wearing head protection
entries.s: You shouldn't _____ your bike without a helmet on busy roads — it's really not safe if you fall and hit your head.
a: ride your bike without a helmet
ride my bike without a helmet
mc: to ride your bike with a helmet

entries.g: nach zehn Uhr nach Hause kommen
entries.d: To arrive at your house later than 10 p.m.
entries.s: My strict parents don't want me to _____ home after ten o'clock at night on school nights — I must be back by 9 pm.
mc: to stay at home after ten

entries.w: to turn your music up loud
entries.d: To increase the volume so everyone can hear
entries.s: Please don't _____ your music up so incredibly loud right now — I'm trying really hard to study for my exam in my bedroom.
mc: to turn your music down quietly

entries.d: To spend an evening out dancing at a club
entries.s: Are you allowed by your parents to _____ to the school disco on Saturday night with loud music and dancing until 11 pm?

entries.w: to play video games all day
entries.d: To spend hours and hours on your console or computer without stopping
entries.s: On rainy boring weekends at home, he really likes to _____ video games all day long from morning until evening on his console.

entries.d: To be in front of the screen later than ten in the evening
entries.s: I'm not allowed to _____ TV after 10 o'clock at night on school nights because mum says I need to sleep.
mc: to watch TV with family on weekends

entries.w: to be allowed to do sth.
entries.d: To have permission to do something
entries.s: We are _____ to use our mobile phones during the 15-minute morning break but not during lessons in the classroom.
mc: to be banned from doing sth.
to be forbidden from doing sth.
to be punished for doing sth.

entries.d: A way to say you are sorry about something disappointing
entries.s: You really can't come to the important football match on Saturday? _____ We really wanted you on the team because we need a goalkeeper.

entries.d: Having many rules and expecting people to follow them
entries.s: Our maths teacher Mrs Schmidt is very _____ about homework deadlines — if you don't hand it in on time, you lose marks.

entries.d: To start using or accepting something new
entries.s: Our school _____ a new rule last September about mobile phones being banned during all school hours, from 8 am to 3 pm.

entries.d: A group of people who live in the same area or share something
entries.s: Everyone in our local _____ helped to clean up the big public park together on Saturday morning, from children to grandparents.

entries.d: Preferring traditional ways and not wanting to change
entries.s: Some _____ elderly people prefer to keep all the old traditions unchanged and don't want anything new or modern to replace them.

entries.d: New machines, computers and other tools we use today
entries.s: _____ like smartphones, laptops, and tablets makes our daily lives easier in many ways by connecting us instantly to information.

entries.d: More than enough, a lot of
entries.s: There is _____ of hot pizza left in the kitchen for everyone at the party — don't worry, we ordered six large pizzas!

entries.d: To talk to God or a higher power
entries.s: Some religious people _____ silently with their hands together before they go to bed at night, thanking God for the day.

entries.d: To make someone suffer because they did something wrong
entries.s: My angry parents _____ me for my bad behaviour by taking away my phone and tablet for a whole week.

entries.d: A way to say the answer changes depending on the situation
entries.s: Are you coming to my party on Saturday? — _____. If it doesn't rain heavily, I'll definitely be there with a present.

entries.d: To not go to bed and remain awake late
entries.s: We _____ awake until after midnight last Friday night watching a really scary horror film on TV with our best friends.

entries.d: To ask someone to come to your house
entries.s: Can I _____ my best friend Tom for dinner tonight? Mum and Dad said it's okay and there will be enough spaghetti.

entries.d: To do something different from what you usually do
entries.s: Let's walk to school today _____ — we always take the lazy bus every day, so it would be nice to try something different for once.

entries.d: A person who writes news for a newspaper, TV or website
entries.s: The _____ from the local newspaper wrote a long interesting article about our school's new science lab last week.

entries.d: Collecting rubbish from the ground outside
entries.s: Our class organised a _____ volunteer day to clean up all the rubbish and plastic bottles in the public park.

entries.d: Not polite, saying or doing things that upset others
entries.s: It's very _____ to talk loudly with your mouth full of food — close your mouth and finish chewing before you speak.

entries.d: So amazing or strange that it is hard to believe
entries.s: The orange and pink sunset over the sea last night was _____! I've never seen anything so beautiful and stunning in my whole life.

entries.d: To be or become very cold
entries.s: I forgot to bring my warm winter jacket to school and almost _____ on the long walk home in the snowy cold wind.

entries.d: To give something to someone for a short time
entries.s: Can you please _____ me your black pen just for this one lesson? I forgot mine at home on my desk and I'll give it back after.

entries.d: A way to say it's OK, don't worry about it
entries.s: Sorry I'm three minutes late for the meeting! — _____! We literally just started one minute ago, so you haven't missed anything.

entries.d: To help someone remember something
entries.s: Please _____ me tomorrow morning to bring my big sports bag for PE class — I always forget on Wednesdays.

entries.d: To organise things or decide which ones to keep
entries.s: I really need to _____ my messy wardrobe this weekend and give away all the old clothes I don't wear anymore.
